dependencies: Large and complex global supply chain
Single suppliers for certain products and materials
Dependence on third-party suppliers and manufacturers
Global supply chain disruptions from COVID-19 pandemic, inflationary pressures, and labor shortages
risks: Disruptions to global supply chain could negatively affect results of operations
Supply chain challenges impacting cost and availability of raw materials, supplies, and services
Single suppliers used for certain products and materials
Manufacturing problems from equipment malfunction, failure to follow protocols, raw material issues, cybersecurity incidents, natural disasters
Dependence on third-party suppliers and manufacturers for product quality and availability

regulatory_risks: FDA and international regulatory agency requirements for product development, approval, manufacturing, labeling, marketing, and post-market surveillance
European Union Medical Devices Regulation (MDR) and In Vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices Regulation (IVDR) imposing additional premarket and post-market requirements
Healthcare fraud and abuse laws including anti-kickback, anti-self-referral, and false claims laws
Data privacy and security regulations including GDPR, China data protection laws, U.S. state laws, and data localization requirements
Physician Payments Sunshine Act and similar transparency laws requiring reporting of transfers of value to healthcare professionals
Trade and investment regulations in many countries requiring local investment or restricting imports
Environmental laws and regulations imposing limitations on emissions and discharges
Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act (Superfund) requirements
Laboratory certification and licensure requirements from CMS, DEA, SAMHSA and foreign counterparts
Medicare and Medicaid reimbursement regulations and competitive bidding systems
WIC program cost containment requirements for infant formula
Potential changes to Affordable Care Act including modification, expansion, or repeal
Tax law changes including OECD two-pillared plan (Pillar 1 and Pillar 2)
Patent and trademark protection challenges and intellectual property invalidation by governments
Clinical trial and post-market study requirements

sanction_risks: Russia's invasion of Ukraine resulting in sanctions, economic and currency volatility, inflation, operational and supply disruptions
Trade protection measures including tariffs, import/export licensing requirements, trade sanctions, and changes to international trade agreements
Potential penalties for violations of anti-corruption, anti-bribery, anti-competition laws including Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and U.K. Bribery Act
